meaning: adj.喜清洁的,干净的,洁净的adv.干净地,清洁地,清白地
examples: These molecules burn more cleanly than gasoline, in part because they have fewer, if and, carbon-carbon bonds, and the hydrocarbons they do emit are less likely to generate ozone.
These molecules burn more cleanly than gasoline, in part because they have fewer, if any, carbon-carbon bonds, and the hydrocarbons they do emit are less likely to generate ozone.
The game had been cleanly fought.
"I am not going to lie and say I caught him cleanly, " said the champion.
It's a question of us as a pack of forwards getting that ball cleanly to them.
